place_results.title: CVS
place_results.description: Drugstore chain selling a variety of beauty & health products, plus some grocery & household items.

places.title: UPS Access Point location
places.address: 300 Park Ave S, New York, NY 10010

places.title: MoneyGram
places.address: Inside Cvs, 750 6th Ave, New York, NY 10010

summary.snippet: "The rudest most horrible pharmacy staff I’ve ever encountered."
"Very nice people work at this location."

most_relevant.description: The staff was incredibly friendly and helpful, making sure I found exactly what I needed. I had an issue with a coupon being rejected by the system, but they quickly addressed it and ensured my purchase went through smoothly while also preventing any future problems. Their positive attitude, attention to detail, and efficiency at the register truly made my visit memorable. The great energy and warm smile made my day.  Keep Smiling.

I visited CVS today, and it’s clear why this is one of the most popular pharmacies in America. You can find a wide range of medications for various health needs, making it very convenient when you’re feeling unwell. In addition to that, they offer a great selection of snacks, drinks, and other everyday essentials, which makes it more than just a pharmacy.

The store has a self-service setup for most products, so you can easily find what you need without waiting in line at the pharmacy counter. The staff were friendly and helpful, which added to the overall experience. While the store could be a bit cleaner, it’s still a great place to shop for both health and daily essentials.

This CVS is pretty big, relatively clean and has all the items we usually find in CVS. The pharmacy is all the way to the back of the store.

Most items are behind locked shelves. At times have to wait for a while for an associate to come over and take the item out. Over the years I have bought cards, small toiletries, snacks from this store. Never had any problem with quality. But they don’t have self checkout here. Most of the time I see long lines for checkout. 2-3 associates always work at register to keep line moving.

This is my local CVS pharmacy.  The good: the non-pharmacy staff are pretty nice and helpful.  The assortment is merchandised well (including visually), is organized and the store stays reasonably in stock.  The bad:  other than the store being overpriced (hard for retail to work in NYC- I get it!), everything is locked up and takes a while to get in and out.  The ugly:  the pharmacy is awful... (a) no way to contact by phone; (b) if there is ever an issue, the staff seems disinterested in finding solutions; (c) they are lazy- there was a problem with a big pharma coupon code - and there was a specific number pharmacies could call to resolve it but refused to be bothered and made me try to navigate it.  There's an alternative across the street that I'm moving my business to- I've had enough!

Purchased an order to print a poster online ahead of time. When I arrived for pickup, the machine was broken and was given direction to send the order to another location. I would recommend calling ahead of the machine is working or having CVS take this off the site before submitting an order.

I had a very disappointing experience today at the CVS Pharmacy on 25th West Street.

The pharmacy closes at 8:00 PM, and I approached the window at 8:01 to ask a simple question about an item’s availability. I politely explained that I only wanted to check if they had the product so I could come back the next day.

Instead of showing even minimal courtesy, a lady at the window bluntly said, “We’re closed.” When I asked again if she could please just check availability, another staff member came over and repeated, “We’re closed, everyone punched out,” and then slammed the door right in front of me.

This type of behavior is completely unacceptable - especially from a pharmacy, where customer care and compassion should come first. It’s disappointing to see such disregard for customers at a healthcare establishment. I hope CVS management at this location addresses this lack of professionalism and reintroduces the basic standards of respect and service that people rightfully expect.
Suzanna at checkout is great! Smart, professional, multitasker and always with a great attitude and smile. She is very helpful and makes shopping at this CVS a pleasure

Cramped store with just about everything locked up or behind security plastic (even the peanuts).  Not a wide variety of food compared to other CVS, and the drink cooler was largely sold out and needed restocked.

live_hash.time_spent: People typically spend 10 min to 1.5 hr here
